title: Stint phase-change features to capture tyre warmup versus degradation cliff
summary: F1 strategy sources emphasize that fresh tyres do not always peak immediately and that degradation is better viewed as a latent evolving state than a fixed linear slope.
rationale: F1 strategy sources emphasize that fresh tyres do not always peak immediately and that degradation is better viewed as a latent evolving state than a fixed linear slope. The current baseline has rolling means but not explicit warmup/cliff regime markers inside a stint.
implementation_hint: Within each Year-Race-Driver-Stint, fit causal piecewise summaries from past laps only: early-stint warmup slope, recent slope acceleration, deviation from expected linear wear, and a binary or continuous 'cliff proximity' score when lap-time residuals and degradation residuals both inflect. Test simple breakpoint heuristics before heavier state-space approximations.
expected_effect: Should improve precision around borderline pit windows where raw tyre age is insufficient, particularly for overcut-capable situations and non-linear compound behavior.
risk: Noisy short stints can create unstable breakpoints; overly complex latent-state estimation may overfit relative to simpler causal slope features.
